notes: Works perfectly on Proton 4 on shooting range and all default maps, colours are accurate, new/positional sound seems to work. Performance is close to on par with native Windows, occasional hiccups, but mostly if ever at the start of the game.
title: Pavlov VR

notes: Out of the box the game runs very well, the only issue is that there seems to be a small amount of screen tearing around the top of the screen that is very noticeable during movement. The performance is nearly on par with it's windows counterpart.
title: Pavlov VR

notes: Renders one frame and then dies. With WineD3d11, all textures except weapons are black, and nothing renders in the headset.
title: Pavlov VR

notes: Had to restart Steam once because SteamVR Home got stuck somehow. It worked perfectly after that. Slight stutters initially, but they went away after a bit.
title: Pavlov VR

notes:
Played this for several hours without any issues related to playing via proton. Default maps run very smoothly.  
A few custom maps seemed to stutter but this might not be a proton issue.
title: Pavlov VR

notes: You may need to reduce graphics settings a bit. I bumped all mine down to medium and it ran like a dream. Certain guns (particularly those with scopes) seem to lag the game a bit for some reason. Also tested on Proton 4.2 and it does NOT work on that version.
title: Pavlov VR

notes: Since proton 4.11 this game runs perfectly out of the box, to get a smooth 120 fps in most areas I had to reduce the settings to medium/low (turning antialiasing off completely looks really bad)
title: Pavlov VR

notes: Runs with solid performance and no noticeable issues without any tweaks.
title: Pavlov VR

notes: Run's perfectly with Valve Index and Valve Index Controllers.  Honestly, it's one of the best VR experiences I've ever had!
title: Pavlov VR
